steps.title: Isolate the fraction term
steps.content:
From $D = T - \frac{9}{25}(100 - H)$, subtract $T$ from both sides:
$$D - T = -\frac{9}{25}(100 - H)$$

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• A: $H = \frac{25}{9}(D - T) + 100$ — matches exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: subtracts 100 instead of adding — sign error.
  • C and D: use $(D + T)$ instead of $(D - T)$ — algebra error.
